data-dictionary-027.easyUnderstanding:
Simple words mein samjho:

Sochho ek company ke paas ek main address book hai — usme sab vendors ke naam, address, bank details likhe hain. Ye address book ek baar banti hai aur bahut kam change hoti hai. Koi naya vendor aaya — ek baar uska record bana diya. Phir hazaaron purchase orders banenge us vendor ke liye — har baar address book se reference lenge, lekin address book copy nahi hoti.

Yahi hai Master Data — reference data jo bahut rarely change hoti hai lekin bahut baar use hoti hai. Vendor master (LFA1/LFB1), Customer master (KNA1/KNB1), Material master (MARA/MARC) — ye sab Master Data hain. Ek baar create hote hain, phir transactions inhe reference karte hain.

Transaction Data alag hai — roz ke kaam ka hisaab. Aaj 500 purchase orders bane, kal 300 invoices — ye daily record hai. Transaction Data tezi se badhta hai aur frequently change hota hai.

Master Data = permanent reference card. Transaction Data = daily diary.
data-dictionary-027.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Master Data SAP ka foundation hai — bina sahi master data ke koi bhi transaction sahi se process nahi ho sakta.

**Master Data ki key characteristics:**
- Long-lived: ek baar create, kaafi time tak valid
- Rarely changes: vendor address change hoti hai kabhi kabhi, roz nahi
- Centrally maintained: ek jagah change karo, sab transactions reflect karte hain
- Referenced by many: ek vendor master hazaaron purchase orders se reference hota hai
- Shared across modules: Material master FI, MM, SD, PP sab mein use hota hai

**Important Master Data tables:**

Vendor Master:
- LFA1 = Vendor general data (naam, address, country) — company-independent
- LFB1 = Vendor company code data (payment terms, reconciliation account) — per company code
- LFM1 = Vendor purchasing data — per purchasing org

Customer Master:
- KNA1 = Customer general data — naam, address
- KNB1 = Customer company code data — payment terms, credit limit
- KNVV = Customer sales area data — pricing, shipping conditions

Material Master:
- MARA = General material data — material type, base unit, weight
- MARC = Plant-specific data — MRP type, procurement type, lead time
- MARD = Storage location stock — actual inventory levels
- MVKE = Sales organization data — sales unit, delivering plant

**Why master data quality matters:**
- Galat vendor bank account = payment goes to wrong place
- Galat material weight in MARA = incorrect freight calculation
- Missing customer credit limit in KNB1 = order blocked unnecessarily
- Wrong plant in MARC = wrong MRP planning

**Master Data vs Transaction Data:**
- Master Data: entity itself — WHO (vendor, customer, material)
- Transaction Data: event — WHAT happened (purchase order created, invoice posted)
- Transaction Data references Master Data — EKKO purchase order header has LIFNR (vendor number) which is foreign key to LFA1
data-dictionary-027.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Master Data is reference data in SAP that is created once and used repeatedly across many business transactions. It represents the entities involved in business processes — vendor master stored in LFA1 for general data and LFB1 for company code-specific data, customer master in KNA1 and KNB1, and material master in MARA for general properties and MARC for plant-specific data.

Master data changes infrequently compared to transaction data — a vendor address might update once a year, but hundreds of purchase orders reference that vendor daily. Master data is also shared across modules — material master MARA is used by MM for procurement, by SD for sales, by PP for production planning, and by FI for valuation.

The quality of master data directly impacts business processes. Incorrect payment terms in LFB1 lead to wrong due date calculations. Missing MRP data in MARC causes planning failures. Wrong reconciliation account in LFB1 causes incorrect FI postings.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein clearly differentiate karo — Master Data = entities (WHO), Transaction Data = events (WHAT happened). Real table names mention karo — LFA1, KNA1, MARA, MARC — ye shows karta hai ki aap sirf concept nahi jaante, actual tables bhi jaante ho. Ek real-world impact example dena bahut effective hota hai.
data-dictionary-027.codeExamples:
* Read vendor master across general and company code tables
* Check material master across MARA and MARC

" Read Vendor Master - joining general and company code data
SELECT a~lifnr, a~name1, a~land1,
       b~bukrs,  b~akont, b~zterm
  FROM lfa1 AS a
  INNER JOIN lfb1 AS b ON a~lifnr = b~lifnr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_vendor)
  WHERE b~bukrs = '1000'
    AND a~lifnr = '100001'.

" Read Material Master - general + plant specific
SELECT a~matnr, a~matkl, a~meins,
       b~werks,  b~dismm, b~lgpla
  FROM mara AS a
  INNER JOIN marc AS b ON a~matnr = b~matnr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_material)
  WHERE b~werks = '1000'.

" Read Customer Master
SELECT a~kunnr, a~name1, a~land1,
       b~bukrs,  b~zterm, b~klimk
  FROM kna1 AS a
  INNER JOIN knb1 AS b ON a~kunnr = b~kunnr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_customer)
  WHERE b~bukrs = '1000'.

followupAnswerBank.question: Why is vendor master split into LFA1 and LFB1? Why not one table?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: LFA1 mein vendor ka general data hai — naam, address, country — jo sab company codes ke liye same hota hai. LFB1 mein company code-specific data hai — payment terms, reconciliation GL account, withholding tax. Ek multinational company mein same vendor ke alag alag company codes mein alag payment terms ho sakti hain. Ek table mein rakhte to company code per row repeat karna padta naam aur address bhi — data redundancy. Split design mein: naam ek baar LFA1 mein, company code data alag LFB1 mein — clean normalized design.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Vendor master is split to avoid data redundancy and support multi-company code scenarios. LFA1 holds vendor-level data that is the same regardless of company code — name, address, language, country. LFB1 holds company code-specific data — payment terms, reconciliation account, withholding tax indicator — which can differ per company code for the same vendor. A single vendor can have different payment terms in company code 1000 (30 days net) versus company code 2000 (60 days net). The split design means name and address are stored once in LFA1, while company code variations are stored as separate LFB1 rows — clean, normalized, no redundancy.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Multi-company project: Global vendor 'ACME Corp' was used across 5 company codes. LFA1 had one record for ACME. LFB1 had 5 records — one per company code with different payment terms, different reconciliation accounts, different withholding tax settings. When ACME changed their address, one update in LFA1 reflected across all 5 company code views automatically.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: LFA1 aur LFB1 ko same samajhna ya sirf LFA1 se query karna aur assume karna ki payment terms bhi wahan hain. Payment terms ZTERM is in LFB1 — not LFA1. Always JOIN both tables for complete vendor information.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Vendor split table structure:
" LFA1: LIFNR, NAME1, LAND1, ORT01 (general - once per vendor)
" LFB1: LIFNR, BUKRS, AKONT, ZTERM (per company code)

SELECT COUNT( DISTINCT a~lifnr ) AS vendors,
       COUNT( b~bukrs )          AS company_assignments
  FROM lfa1 AS a
  LEFT JOIN lfb1 AS b ON a~lifnr = b~lifnr
  INTO @DATA(ls_count).

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between MARA and MARC in material master?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: MARA = material ka aisa data jo sab plants ke liye same hai — material number, material type, base unit of measure, weight, volume, industry sector. MARC = plant-specific data — har plant mein same material ka alag planning data ho sakta hai. Jaise Delhi plant mein material ka MRP type 'PD' (MRP with planned orders) ho sakta hai lekin Mumbai plant mein 'VB' (reorder point planning). Plant-specific lead time, procurement type, special procurement key — sab MARC mein. Ek material ka ek MARA row hoga — bahut MARC rows ho sakte hain (ek per plant jahan material extend hua ho).
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: MARA holds general material data independent of plant — material type, industry sector, base unit of measure, gross weight, net weight, material group. This data is the same regardless of which plant the material exists in. MARC holds plant-specific material data — one record per material-plant combination. Plant-specific data includes MRP type, MRP controller, lot sizing procedure, planned delivery time, procurement type, special procurement key, and ABC indicator. The same material might be produced in plant 1000 but purchased externally in plant 2000 — MARC.BESKZ differs per plant. When extending a material to a new plant in MM01, a new MARC record is created.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production planning issue: Material M001 existed in two plants. Plant 1000 MARC had MRP type PD (deterministic MRP), plant 2000 MARC had MRP type ND (no planning). Demand signal in plant 2000 was generating no planned orders — investigated MARC for plant 2000, found MRP type ND set by mistake. Corrected to VB. MRP now running correctly for both plants from same MARA material.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: MARA se query karna aur assume karna ki plant-specific data milega. MARA mein plant-specific fields nahi hain. MRP type, lead time, procurement type ke liye MARC join karna zaroori hai. SELECT FROM mara JOIN marc — always pair these.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" MARA: material exists once (company-independent)
" MARC: one record per material-plant combination

" Count how many plants a material is extended to:
SELECT matnr, COUNT( werks ) AS plant_count
  FROM marc
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_ext)
  WHERE matnr = 'MAT001'
  GROUP BY matnr.

" MRP data from MARC:
SELECT matnr, werks, dismm, dzeit, beskz
  FROM marc
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mrp)
  WHERE werks IN ('1000', '2000').

followupAnswerBank.question: How is master data different from configuration data in SAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Master data = business entities — vendors, customers, materials. Business users maintain karte hain — MM01, XK01, etc. Configuration data = system settings — payment terms definition, plant settings, company code settings. Functional consultants IMG mein maintain karte hain. Master data typically client-dependent application data (delivery class A). Configuration data typically delivery class C (customizing) — SM30 se transport hota hai. Master data rarely transport hota hai directly — usually manually created ya migrated via LSMW/BAPI. Config data transport mechanism se system-to-system jaata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Master data represents business entities — vendors created via XK01, customers via XD01, materials via MM01. It is maintained by business users in operational transactions. Configuration data represents system settings — payment terms defined in OBB8, plants in SPRO, company codes in OX02. Configuration is maintained by functional consultants in IMG. From a Data Dictionary perspective: master data tables have delivery class A — application data, not transported via transport requests. Configuration tables have delivery class C — transported via customizing requests from IMG. Master data moves between systems via data migration tools like LSMW, BAPIs, or data migration workbench — not via transport requests.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live checklist item: Client asked 'Will vendor master from QA be available in production?' Answer: No — vendor master has delivery class A, it does not transport. All 2,000 vendor records needed to be migrated from legacy system to production directly via BAPI_VENDOR_SAVE or LSMW. Configuration data (payment terms, house banks) was already there via transport.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Assume karna ki vendor master ya customer master transport ho jaayega QA se production. Master data delivery class A hai — transport nahi hoti. Data migration project alag hota hai configuration transport se. Interview mein clearly distinguish karo in dono ke between.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check delivery class of master data tables:
SELECT tabname, contflag
  FROM dd02l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_cls)
  WHERE tabname IN ('LFA1', 'LFB1', 'KNA1', 'MARA')
    AND as4local = 'A'.
" contflag = 'A' for all: Application data, not transported

" Master data migration via BAPI:
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_VENDOR_SAVE'
  EXPORTING
    lifnr          = '100001'
    vendorgeneral  = ls_general
    vendorcompany  = ls_company.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the significance of the material type in material master?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Material type (MARA-MTART) decide karta hai material ka behavior — kahan use ho sakta hai, kaise valuate hoga, kaunse views mandatory hain. FERT = finished goods — own production. HALB = semifinished product. ROH = raw material — purchased. HAWA = trading goods — bought aur sold as-is. DIEN = service. Material type procurement type decide karta hai (internal/external), valuation class determine karta hai (which GL account gets hit), aur MRP applicability control karta hai. Galat material type = galat GL posting, wrong planning.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Material type (MTART in MARA) is a master data attribute that controls material behavior throughout SAP. It determines which organizational levels the material can be maintained for, which screens appear during material master creation, the allowed procurement type (internal production, external purchase, or both), the default valuation class used in FI-MM integration, and whether quantity and value updates occur in inventory management. For example, FERT (finished goods) defaults to internal procurement and specific valuation class. ROH (raw material) defaults to external procurement. Using the wrong material type causes incorrect GL account determination during goods movements and incorrect MRP planning.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Data error: 50 materials were created as HAWA (trading goods) but should have been FERT (finished goods). MRP was not generating production orders for these materials — HAWA defaults to external procurement. Corrective action: material type change via MMAM transaction. Changed MTART from HAWA to FERT — MRP then generated production orders correctly.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Material type randomly choose karna without understanding its impact. Each material type maps to specific valuation class, procurement type, and allowed transactions. Wrong material type discovered late causes expensive data corrections. Always confirm with functional consultant before creating materials.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Material types and procurement:
SELECT matnr, mtart, matkl, meins
  FROM mara
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mat)
  WHERE mtart IN ('FERT', 'ROH', 'HAWA')
    AND matnr LIKE 'MAT%'.

" Check valuation class from MARC+MBEW:
SELECT a~matnr, a~werks, b~bklas
  FROM marc AS a
  INNER JOIN mbew AS b
    ON a~matnr = b~matnr AND a~werks = b~bwkey
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_val)
  WHERE a~werks = '1000'.

followupAnswerBank.question: What are the key tables for customer master and what data is in each?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: KNA1 = customer general data — customer number, name, address, language, country. Ek record per customer, company-independent. KNB1 = customer company code data — reconciliation account, payment terms, credit limit — ek record per customer per company code. KNVV = customer sales area data — pricing procedure, customer group, delivering plant, incoterms, shipping conditions — ek record per customer per sales org + distribution channel + division combination. SD module ke liye KNVV important hai. FI module ke liye KNB1 important hai. General reporting ke liye KNA1.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Customer master is organized in three main layers. KNA1 contains general customer data — customer number (KUNNR), name, address, country, language, tax number — stored once regardless of company or sales area. KNB1 contains company code-specific data — reconciliation account (AKONT), payment terms (ZTERM), credit limit, dunning procedure — one record per customer per company code. KNVV contains sales area-specific data — customer pricing procedure, customer group, incoterms, delivering plant, shipping conditions — one record per customer per combination of sales organization, distribution channel, and division. A customer active in 2 company codes and 3 sales areas would have 1 KNA1 record, 2 KNB1 records, and 3 KNVV records.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SD customization: New sales area (sales org 1000, distribution channel 20, division 00) created. Existing customers not appearing in sales orders for new sales area. Root cause: KNVV records not created for new sales area. Used XD01 to extend customers to new sales area — created KNVV records. After extension, customers available in new sales area.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Customer reporting ke liye sirf KNA1 query karna aur assume karna ki payment terms wahan hain. Payment terms ZTERM is in KNB1. Pricing procedure, delivering plant is in KNVV. Always JOIN appropriately based on what business data you need.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Complete customer read across 3 master tables:
SELECT a~kunnr, a~name1, a~land1,
       b~zterm, b~akont, b~klimk,
       v~vkorg, v~kdgrp, v~inco1
  FROM kna1 AS a
  INNER JOIN knb1 AS b ON a~kunnr = b~kunnr
  INNER JOIN knvv AS v ON a~kunnr = v~kunnr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_cust)
  WHERE b~bukrs = '1000'
    AND v~vkorg = '1000'.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you maintain master data — what transactions are used?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Master data maintain karne ke liye dedicated transactions hain. Vendor: XK01 create, XK02 change, XK03 display — cross-company code. MK01/MK02 purchasing organization specific. Customer: XD01 create, XD02 change, XD03 display. VD01/VD02 sales-specific. Material: MM01 create, MM02 change, MM03 display. MMAM material type change. Business user directly ye transactions use karta hai. ABAP developer BAPI ya direct table write se mass creation karta hai. Mass maintenance: MASS transaction — multiple records ek saath change.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Master data is maintained through dedicated SAP transactions. Vendor master: XK01 (create), XK02 (change), XK03 (display) for central maintenance including purchasing and accounting views. MK01/MK02 for purchasing-only view. FK01/FK02 for accounting-only view. Customer master: XD01 (create), XD02 (change), XD03 (display) centrally. VD01/VD02 for sales view. FD01/FD02 for finance view. Material master: MM01 (create), MM02 (change), MM03 (display). MMAM for material type change. For mass changes: MASS transaction allows changing one field across multiple records simultaneously. For batch uploads: LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench) or BAPIs like BAPI_VENDOR_SAVE, BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Mass vendor creation task: 500 vendors needed to be created for a new plant go-live. Manual XK01 creation would take weeks. Used LSMW with BAPI_VENDOR_SAVE — created batch input session that created all 500 vendors in 2 hours. Data was provided in Excel, converted to CSV, loaded via LSMW direct input method.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Directly INSERT INTO lfa1 karne ki koshish karna vendor create karne ke liye. Direct table insert breaks many downstream dependencies — number ranges, change documents, ALE distribution. Always use standard BAPIs or transactions for master data creation.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Mass vendor creation via BAPI:
DATA: ls_head  TYPE bapi_vendor_head,
      lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapireturn1.

ls_head-comp_code   = '1000'.
ls_head-acct_group  = 'KRED'.
ls_head-name        = 'Test Vendor GmbH'.
ls_head-country     = 'DE'.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_VENDOR_SAVE'
  EXPORTING  vendorgeneral = ls_head
  TABLES     return        = lt_return.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of account groups in vendor and customer master?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Account group vendor master mein bahut important setting hai jo decide karta hai: number range (vendor number kaunse range se aayega — internal ya external), which fields are mandatory/optional/hidden (domestic vendor ke liye alag, foreign vendor ke liye alag fields), which organizational levels are relevant (company code, purchasing org assignment). KRED = standard domestic vendor. LIEF = Lieferant (another vendor group). One-time vendors ka alag account group hota hai (CRED) — address per document enter hota hai, fixed address nahi hota. Similarly customer account groups control customer numbering, mandatory fields, and whether it is a one-time customer.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Account groups in vendor and customer master data control three key aspects. First, number assignment — whether the master record number is assigned internally by SAP from a number range, or entered externally by the user. Second, field selection — which fields are required, optional, suppressed, or display-only during master data maintenance. Different account groups can have different field requirements — a foreign vendor might require bank country and IBAN fields that are optional for domestic vendors. Third, partner determination — certain account groups are used for special partner types like one-time vendors (CRED account group) where address is entered per document transaction rather than stored in master data. Wrong account group assignment causes wrong number ranges and incorrect field requirements.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Configuration issue: New vendor created with account group KRED (domestic). Vendor was actually foreign — IBAN field was optional for KRED but should have been mandatory. Payments failed due to missing IBAN. Fix: vendor re-created with correct foreign vendor account group where IBAN was required field. Account group cannot be changed after creation in most cases — requires deletion and recreation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Account group as a trivial setting treat karna. Wrong account group = wrong number range, wrong mandatory fields, potential payment failures. Always confirm account group with functional consultant before creating master data in production.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check vendor account groups:
SELECT lifnr, ktokk, land1, name1
  FROM lfa1
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_vend)
  WHERE ktokk = 'KRED'
    AND land1 = 'DE'.

" Number ranges for vendor account groups:
" T-code: XKN1 - vendor number ranges
" T-code: XDN1 - customer number ranges

" Account group stored in:
" LFA1-KTOKK for vendors
" KNA1-KTOKD for customers

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a one-time vendor or customer and how is it handled in SAP master data?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: One-time vendor ya customer wo hota hai jiske saath ek baar kaam karo aur phir dobara shayad nahi. Jaise ek conference venue jahan ek event ke liye payment ki — ye vendor dobara use nahi hoga. Har aisi vendor ke liye full master data banana wastes effort aur pollutes master data. SAP solution: ek single generic 'one-time vendor' master record — CRED account group. Is master record mein blank address fields hain. Jab purchase order ya invoice banate hain is vendor se — us specific transaction pe actual vendor address enter karo. Har transaction different actual vendor ho sakta hai — sab same 'CRED vendor' LIFNR ke through.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: One-time vendors (account group CRED in standard SAP) are a special master data pattern for infrequent or one-off suppliers. Instead of creating a full vendor master record for each unique vendor, a single generic master record is created with the account group set to one-time. The address and bank details fields in this master record are left blank. When a purchasing document (purchase order or invoice) is created referencing this one-time vendor, SAP prompts for the vendor's address at the document level — the details are stored on the document, not in the master record. This keeps master data clean and avoids creating thousands of rarely-used vendor records. One-time customer equivalent exists for SD transactions.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Accounts payable cleanup: Client had 3,000 vendor records — analysis showed 2,100 were used only once in the last 5 years. These should have been processed as one-time vendors. Master data rationalization project moved future one-off vendors to one-time vendor process. Reduced vendor master size by 70% over 2 years, improving system performance and master data quality.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Har vendor ke liye full master data banana even for single transactions. This bloats LFA1 with thousands of inactive records, complicates vendor searches, and creates data maintenance overhead. One-time vendor pattern specifically exists for this scenario.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" One-time vendor: account group CRED
SELECT lifnr, name1, ktokk
  FROM lfa1
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_onetm)
  WHERE ktokk = 'CRED'.
" Typically shows only 1-3 generic one-time vendor records

" Document-level address stored in BSEC:
SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, name1, ort01
  FROM bsec  " One-time account document segment
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_addr)
  WHERE bukrs = '1000'.

data-dictionary-028.easyUnderstanding:
Simple words mein samjho:

Master Data ek permanent address book hai — kabhi kabhi badhta hai, rarely change hota hai. Ab sochho roz ka business: aaj 200 purchase orders aaye, 150 goods receipts hue, 100 invoices post hue. Ye sab events hain — aaj ka kaam, kal alag events honge. Ye sab Transaction Data hai.

Transaction Data = business ka diary. Har entry ek event record karti hai — kab hua, kya hua, kitna hua, kaunse parties involved the. Purchase order (EKKO/EKPO) = kya khareedna hai order. Goods receipt (MKPF/MSEG) = kab maal aaya. Invoice (BKPF/BSEG) = payment ke liye. Sales order (VBAK/VBAP) = customer ko kya bechna hai.

Transaction Data ki characteristics:
- Roz create hoti hai, high volume
- Ek baar post ho gayi to rarely change hoti (audit trail)
- Master data ko reference karti hai (vendor number, material number)
- Time-stamped — kab hua exactly

Transaction Data = daily ledger. Badhta jaata hai, historical record ban jaata hai.
data-dictionary-028.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Transaction Data SAP ka operational heart hai — sab business events yahan record hote hain.

**Key Transaction Data table pairs (Header + Item):**

Procurement (MM):
- EKKO = Purchase Order Header — document number, vendor, order date, purchasing org
- EKPO = Purchase Order Item — material, quantity, price, delivery date, plant
- MKPF = Goods Movement Header — document number, posting date, movement type
- MSEG = Goods Movement Item — material, quantity, storage location, account assignment

Financials (FI):
- BKPF = Accounting Document Header — company code, fiscal year, document type, posting date
- BSEG = Accounting Document Item — GL account, debit/credit, amount, cost center
- In S/4HANA: ACDOCA replaces the function of BKPF+BSEG combination

Sales (SD):
- VBAK = Sales Order Header — customer, sales org, order date, pricing date
- VBAP = Sales Order Item — material, quantity, net value, plant, storage location
- LIKP = Delivery Header — ship-to party, delivery date, route
- LIPS = Delivery Item — material, delivered quantity
- VBRK = Billing Header — billing type, customer, billing date
- VBRP = Billing Item — material, billed quantity, net value

**Transaction Data characteristics:**
- Header + Item pattern: EKKO has basic info, EKPO has line details
- Document number + fiscal year: natural key for financial documents
- Immutable after posting: business integrity — past transactions should not change
- High volume: a busy company posts thousands of documents daily
- Historical growth: grows every day, never shrinks (archiving aside)
- Foreign keys to master data: EKKO-LIFNR references LFA1, EKPO-MATNR references MARA

**Why Header-Item design:**
- EKKO has one row per purchase order — 10 fields common to all items
- EKPO has one row per item — material, quantity, price per line
- If all in one table: document-level info repeated for every item — data redundancy
- Join EKKO + EKPO on EBELN (document number) for full picture
data-dictionary-028.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Transaction Data in SAP records every business event — purchase orders in EKKO and EKPO, goods movements in MKPF and MSEG, accounting postings in BKPF and BSEG, and sales orders in VBAK and VBAP. Unlike master data which is created once and reused, transaction data is generated continuously with every business operation.

Transaction data tables follow a header-item pattern — the header table stores document-level information common to all items, and the item table stores line-specific details. EKKO stores one row per purchase order with vendor and date information, while EKPO stores one row per purchase order line item with material, quantity, and price.

Transaction data references master data via foreign keys — EKKO.LIFNR points to LFA1 vendor master, EKPO.MATNR points to MARA material master. This is why master data quality directly impacts transaction processing — a wrong vendor bank account in LFB1 causes payment failures even after thousands of correct purchase orders were processed.'

Hinglish tip: Table pairs clearly mention karo — EKKO/EKPO, BKPF/BSEG, VBAK/VBAP, MKPF/MSEG. Ye shows karta hai ki aap real SAP tables jaante ho. Header-Item pattern explain karna ek strong interview answer point hai.
data-dictionary-028.codeExamples:
* Read purchase orders joining header and item
* Read accounting documents
* Read sales orders

" Purchase Order: header + items
SELECT h~ebeln, h~lifnr, h~bedat,
       i~ebelp, i~matnr, i~menge, i~netpr
  FROM ekko AS h
  INNER JOIN ekpo AS i ON h~ebeln = i~ebeln
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_po)
  WHERE h~bukrs = '1000'
    AND h~bedat GE '20240101'.

" Accounting documents: BKPF + BSEG
SELECT h~bukrs, h~belnr, h~gjahr, h~bldat,
       i~buzei, i~hkont, i~dmbtr, i~shkzg
  FROM bkpf AS h
  INNER JOIN bseg AS i
    ON  h~bukrs = i~bukrs
    AND h~belnr = i~belnr
    AND h~gjahr = i~gjahr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_fi)
  WHERE h~bukrs = '1000'
    AND h~gjahr = '2024'.

" Sales order items:
SELECT vbeln, posnr, matnr, kwmeng, netpr
  FROM vbap
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items)
  WHERE vbeln = '0000012345'.

followupAnswerBank.question: Why do transaction data tables use a header-item design pattern?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Header-Item pattern data redundancy avoid karta hai. Ek purchase order mein 50 line items ho sakte hain — vendor number, order date, company code sab 50 items ke liye same hoga. Ek table mein rakhte to vendor number 50 times repeat hota. Header mein ek row, items mein 50 rows — vendor number sirf ek baar. Data storage efficient hota hai aur update bhi easy — vendor change karna ho to sirf EKKO mein ek row update karo, sabke liye reflect hoga.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The header-item design pattern avoids data redundancy and enables efficient storage. A purchase order may have 50 line items, but information like vendor number, order date, company code, and purchasing organization is the same for all 50 items. Storing everything in one table would repeat this header data 50 times — wasted storage and consistency risk. The header-item split stores document-level data once in the header table (EKKO) and line-specific data in the item table (EKPO). The two are linked via the document number (EBELN). This pattern also enables efficient reporting — to find all purchase orders for a vendor, query only EKKO without touching the larger item table.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance optimization: Custom report was joining EKKO and EKPO unnecessarily for a summary that only needed header-level data (vendor, date, document number). By removing the EKPO join and reading only from EKKO, query runtime reduced from 45 seconds to 3 seconds. EKKO had 500,000 rows; EKPO had 3,000,000 rows. Unnecessary join multiplied rows processed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Ek simple header-level report ke liye bhi item table join karna. Pehle check karo — kya mujhe item-level data chahiye? Agar sirf document number, vendor, date chahiye — EKKO alone enough. Join only when item fields (material, quantity, price) are needed.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Header-only query (efficient - no item join):
SELECT ebeln, lifnr, bedat, netwr
  FROM ekko
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_headers)
  WHERE lifnr = '100001'
    AND bedat GE '20240101'.

" Only join items when item data needed:
SELECT h~ebeln, i~matnr, i~menge
  FROM ekko AS h
  INNER JOIN ekpo AS i ON h~ebeln = i~ebeln
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_detail)
  WHERE h~lifnr = '100001'.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the significance of BKPF and BSEG and how do they work together?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: BKPF = Accounting Document Header. Ek accounting document ka summary — company code, fiscal year, document number, posting date, document type, user who posted. BSEG = Accounting Document Item — har line item ka detail. Debit GL account, credit GL account, amount, cost center, profit center, tax code. Ek invoice document mein BKPF pe 1 row, BSEG pe 3-4 rows (vendor line, expense line, tax line). BKPF ka primary key: MANDT + BUKRS + BELNR + GJAHR. BSEG ka primary key: same plus BUZEI (line item number). S/4HANA mein ACDOCA has replaced much of this — BSEG still accessible via compatibility.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: BKPF is the accounting document header — one record per financial document, storing company code (BUKRS), document number (BELNR), fiscal year (GJAHR), document type (BLART), posting date (BUDAT), and entry user. BSEG is the accounting document line item table — one record per line item in each document, identified by adding BUZEI (line item number) to the header key. BSEG stores the actual debit/credit entries: GL account (HKONT), debit/credit indicator (SHKZG), amount in local currency (DMBTR), cost center, profit center, tax code. An invoice document might have one BKPF record and three BSEG records: vendor payable line, expense line, and tax line. In S/4HANA, ACDOCA is the primary storage but BSEG remains accessible for compatibility.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Audit report: Need all payments to a specific vendor in FY2024. Approach: SELECT FROM bkpf WHERE blart = 'ZP' (payment doc type) JOIN bseg WHERE lifnr = vendor. BKPF gave the date and document number, BSEG gave the amount and bank GL account. Combined to produce payment listing with dates, amounts, and bank accounts used.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: BKPF se amount try karna — BKPF mein total amount nahi hoti (HWAE field is currency only). Actual debit/credit amounts BSEG mein hain. BKPF gives document metadata, BSEG gives line item financial data.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Complete FI document read:
SELECT h~belnr, h~budat, h~blart,
       i~buzei, i~hkont, i~shkzg,
       i~dmbtr, i~lifnr
  FROM bkpf AS h
  INNER JOIN bseg AS i
    ON  h~mandt = i~mandt
    AND h~bukrs = i~bukrs
    AND h~belnr = i~belnr
    AND h~gjahr = i~gjahr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_fi)
  WHERE h~bukrs = '1000'
    AND h~gjahr  = '2024'
    AND h~blart  = 'RE'.  " Invoice documents

followupAnswerBank.question: How is goods movement data structured in MKPF and MSEG?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: MKPF = Material Document Header — warehouse/inventory movement ka header. Mandt, MBLNR (material document number), MJAHR (material document year), BUDAT (posting date), USNAM (user). MSEG = Material Document Item — har goods movement line. Movement type (BWART) — 101 = goods receipt against PO, 601 = goods issue for delivery, 261 = goods issue for production order. Material (MATNR), quantity (MENGE), plant (WERKS), storage location (LGORT). Har inventory transaction (goods receipt, goods issue, transfer posting) MKPF + MSEG combination create karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: MKPF is the material document header for inventory movements — one record per goods movement transaction, storing material document number (MBLNR), material document year (MJAHR), posting date, and user. MSEG is the material document item — one record per material affected in the movement, storing movement type (BWART), material (MATNR), quantity (MENGE), plant (WERKS), storage location (LGORT), and the reference document (purchase order via EBELN/EBELP, or production order via AUFNR). Movement type is key: 101 = goods receipt against purchase order, 102 = reversal, 261 = goods issue for production order, 601 = goods issue for customer delivery. Stock levels in MARD are updated based on MSEG postings.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Reconciliation task: Find all goods receipts for PO 4500012345. Query: SELECT FROM mseg WHERE ebeln = '4500012345' AND bwart = '101'. Results gave MBLNR and MJAHR of material documents. Joined with MKPF for posting dates. Compared total received quantity with ordered quantity in EKPO-MENGE — found 10 units short received.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: MENGE field in MSEG without VOYME field — for reversal documents (102) MENGE is positive but SHKZG or BWART indicates it is a reversal. Always check BWART to determine if movement increases or decreases stock.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Goods receipts for a purchase order:
SELECT h~mblnr, h~mjahr, h~budat,
       i~matnr, i~menge, i~werks, i~lgort
  FROM mkpf AS h
  INNER JOIN mseg AS i
    ON  h~mandt = i~mandt
    AND h~mblnr = i~mblnr
    AND h~mjahr = i~mjahr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_gr)
  WHERE i~ebeln = '4500012345'
    AND i~bwart = '101'.  " GR against PO

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the structure of sales order data in VBAK and VBAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: VBAK = Sales Order Header (V = Vertrieb/Sales, B = Beleg/Document, A = Auftrag/Order, K = Kopf/Header). VBELN = sales document number, KUNNR = sold-to customer, VKORG = sales org, VTWEG = distribution channel, VKGRP = sales group, AUART = order type. VBAP = Sales Order Item (P = Position/Item). VBELN same document number, POSNR = item number (000010, 000020 etc.), MATNR = material, KWMENG = confirmed quantity, NETPR = net price, WERKS = delivering plant. Reports joining VBAK + VBAP on VBELN give complete order picture.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: VBAK is the sales order header table — one record per sales order. Key fields: VBELN (sales document number, 10 digits), KUNNR (sold-to customer), VKORG (sales organization), VTWEG (distribution channel), SPART (division), AUART (sales document type like OR, ZOR), ERDAT (creation date). VBAP is the sales order item table — one record per item. Key fields: VBELN (same document number), POSNR (item number in increments of 10), MATNR (material), KWMENG (confirmed quantity), NETPR (net price), WERKS (delivering plant), LGORT (storage location), PSTYV (item category). Together they give the full order — VBAK for who ordered what when, VBAP for what exactly was ordered per line.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Open order backlog report: SELECT FROM vbak JOIN vbap WHERE GBSTA (overall status) not equal 'C' (completed). Filter AUART for relevant order types. Result gave all open orders with customer, material, quantities, and delivering plant — used for production planning input and delivery scheduling.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: VBAK mein total order value dhundhna — VBAK does not have item-level amounts. VBAP-NETPR is net price per item, VBAP-KWMENG is quantity. Calculate value as NETPR * KWMENG at item level. VBAK-NETWR is header-level total but use with caution.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Open sales order report:
SELECT h~vbeln, h~kunnr, h~erdat, h~auart,
       i~posnr, i~matnr, i~kwmeng, i~netpr
  FROM vbak AS h
  INNER JOIN vbap AS i ON h~vbeln = i~vbeln
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_so)
  WHERE h~vkorg = '1000'
    AND h~auart = 'OR'
    AND i~abgru = ' '  " No rejection reason
  ORDER BY h~erdat DESCENDING.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does transaction data volume affect database design and performance?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Transaction Data ek direction mein badhti rehti hai — never shrinks (archiving ke bina). Ek company mein BSEG daily lakhs rows add kar sakta hai. Saal ke end pe BSEG mein billions of rows ho sakti hain. Performance impact: SELECT on BSEG without good WHERE clause = full table scan on billion-row table = system crash risk. Design decisions: BKPF+BSEG join pe BUKRS aur GJAHR always filter karo — ye primary key ke part hain. Date range filters lagao. Large transaction tables pe secondary indexes carefully choose karo. S/4HANA mein ACDOCA ek hi large table — HANA column store se manage hoti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Transaction data grows unboundedly and accumulates historical records — a company operating for 10 years has 10 years of BSEG, MSEG, and VBAP records. This volume directly impacts query performance. Reports without selective WHERE conditions on high-volume tables cause full table scans — in a BSEG with 2 billion rows, a full scan is unacceptable. Best practices: always filter on BUKRS and GJAHR when querying BSEG since they are part of the primary key. Filter on BUDAT range rather than open-ended queries. For MSEG, filter on MJAHR and movement type. Use ST05 to check query execution plans and verify indexes are being used. Archiving (via SARA transaction) is used to remove historical transaction data that is no longer operationally required.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance incident: Year-end report query on BSEG ran for 6 hours. Analysis: WHERE clause had only BUDAT filter — no BUKRS or GJAHR. BSEG has composite primary key MANDT+BUKRS+BELNR+GJAHR+BUZEI — BUDAT is not in the key. Index was not being used. Fix: Added WHERE bukrs = '1000' AND gjahr = '2024' — query runtime dropped to 4 minutes.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Transaction tables pe WHERE clause mein primary key fields nahi daalna. BKPF/BSEG mein always BUKRS aur GJAHR filter karo. EKKO/EKPO mein BUKRS filter karo. VBAK mein VKORG filter helpful. Primary key pe filtering = index scan = fast.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Correct: selective query on high-volume BSEG
SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, buzei, hkont, dmbtr
  FROM bseg
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_bseg)
  WHERE bukrs = '1000'   " Primary key field - selective
    AND gjahr = '2024'   " Primary key field - selective
    AND hkont = '300000'  " GL account filter
    AND budat GE '20240101' AND budat LE '20241231'.

" Wrong: missing primary key fields (full table scan risk)
" WHERE budat GE '20240101'  " Only date - full scan!

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the relationship between purchase order, goods receipt, and invoice in table terms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Procurement cycle teen transactions hain — 3-way match ke liye. Purchase Order: EKKO/EKPO. Goods Receipt: MKPF/MSEG — MSEG mein EBELN aur EBELP fields hain jo PO number aur PO item refer karte hain. Invoice: RSEG (invoice item) mein EBELN aur EBELP hain jo PO reference dete hain. EKBE table = purchase order history — ek tracking table jo PO ke against sab movements track karta hai (receipt quantity, invoice quantity, return quantity). EKBE se pata chalta hai ek PO kitna receive hua, kitna invoiced hua. 3-way match: PO quantity = GR quantity = invoice quantity — tino match to payment approved.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The procurement cycle creates linked transaction documents traceable through table relationships. Purchase order (EKKO/EKPO) has EBELN as its key. When goods receipt happens against the PO, MSEG stores EBELN and EBELP linking the material document back to the PO. When the vendor invoice is posted, RSEG (invoice verification item) stores the EBELN and EBELP reference. The EKBE table is the purchase order history table — it summarizes all movements against a PO: quantities received (movement type 101), quantities invoiced (movement type WE = goods receipt, RE = invoice), and return deliveries. Matching PO quantity (EKPO-MENGE) with received quantity (EKBE quantity for GR) and invoiced quantity (EKBE quantity for IR) is the automated 3-way match in SAP.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Invoice block investigation: Vendor invoice blocked for payment. Used EKBE to check PO history — PO ordered 100 units, GR showed 80 units received, invoice was for 100 units. Invoice blocked because goods receipt quantity did not match invoice quantity — SAP 3-way match detected the discrepancy. Correct action: wait for remaining 20 units delivery before full invoice payment.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Directly joining MSEG and RBKP (invoice header) without going through PO — complex and unreliable. Use EKBE as the reconciliation point — it is specifically designed to show PO history across GR, IR, and returns in a consolidated view.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" PO history via EKBE:
SELECT ebeln, ebelp, vgabe, menge, dmbtr
  FROM ekbe
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_hist)
  WHERE ebeln = '4500012345'.
" vgabe: 1=GR, 2=Invoice, 3=Return, 4=Credit memo

" 3-way match check:
SELECT po~menge AS po_qty,
       SUM( CASE gr~vgabe WHEN '1' THEN gr~menge ELSE 0 END ) AS gr_qty
  FROM ekpo AS po
  LEFT JOIN ekbe AS gr ON po~ebeln = gr~ebeln AND po~ebelp = gr~ebelp
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_match)
  WHERE po~ebeln = '4500012345'
  GROUP BY po~menge.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is document number range and how does it work for transaction data?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Document number range define karta hai transaction document numbers kaise assign hote hain. Accounting documents ke liye: company code per fiscal year per document type ek number range hoti hai. FI invoice document type RE — number range 19 — 1900000001 se 1999999999 tak. SAP internally next available number assign karta hai (internal number range) ya user manually enter karta hai (external number range). Number range tables: NRIV — number range intervals, object-wise. Custom objects ke liye custom number range define karna padta hai via transaction SNRO.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Document number ranges define the numbering scheme for transaction data documents. Each document type in FI has an assigned number range interval — when a document is posted, SAP assigns the next available number from that range for the current fiscal year and company code. Internal number ranges are automatically assigned by SAP; external number ranges require the user to enter the document number manually. Number ranges are managed by number range objects — FI uses object RF_BELEG, MM uses object MATERIALDOC for material documents. The key tables are NRIV (number range intervals) and NRIK (current number status). Running out of number range causes posting failures — number range must be extended before exhaustion.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Year-end task: Year-end procedures include verifying that document number ranges are extended for the new fiscal year. For FI, number ranges are year-dependent — each new fiscal year needs its own range or carried over from previous year. One client forgot to extend number ranges — first posting on January 1 failed with 'Number range interval not found' error. Emergency: SNRO/FBN1 to extend ranges, then repost.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Number range extension ko year-end checklist mein include nahi karna. FI document number ranges are fiscal-year dependent — must be extended for new year. Set up calendar reminder or automated check to verify number ranges before fiscal year change.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check number range status for FI:
" T-code FBN1 - FI document number ranges
" T-code SNRO - general number range maintenance

" ABAP: check current number range status
SELECT nrobj, nrniv, fromnumber, tonumber, nrlevel
  FROM nriv
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_nr)
  WHERE nrobj = 'RF_BELEG'  " FI document number object
    AND nrniv = '19'.       " Number range interval
" nrlevel = current last used number
" Alert if (tonumber - nrlevel) < 1000000 (running low)

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between posting date and document date in transaction data?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: BKPF mein do dates hain jo confuse karte hain. BLDAT = Document Date — vendor invoice pe jo date printed hai — vendor ne kab invoice issue kiya. BUDAT = Posting Date — SAP mein document kab post kiya gaya — accounting period determine karta hai. Ek invoice December 28 ki date pe aai lekin January 5 ko process ki — BLDAT December 28, BUDAT January 5. Ye important hai: BUDAT decide karta hai kaunse period mein GL entry jaayegi. Agar period December close ho gaya to BUDAT January dena padega. Reports mein BUDAT use karo period-based GL reporting ke liye, BLDAT use karo vendor invoice date tracking ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In BKPF, BLDAT is the document date — the date printed on the original source document, such as the vendor invoice issue date or the delivery note date. BUDAT is the posting date — the date on which the document is posted in SAP, which determines the accounting period for the GL entry. These dates differ when there is a lag between document receipt and processing. A vendor invoice dated December 28 received on January 3 and processed on January 5 would have BLDAT = December 28 and BUDAT = January 5 (next fiscal period). The posting date controls period determination in FI — if December is already closed, the BUDAT must be in January. For financial reporting by period, always use BUDAT. For tracking original document dates, use BLDAT.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Audit question: 'Was this vendor invoice posted in the correct accounting period?' Analysis: checked BKPF-BUDAT — posting date was January 3 (period 1 of new year). BLDAT was December 28 (period 12 of old year). Invoice was received after period 12 closed — correct to post in January. Audit confirmed: correct accounting treatment.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: BLDAT aur BUDAT mix up karna in reports. Agar period-based financial report banana hai — BUDAT use karo. BLDAT use karna for aging reports (how old is the invoice since vendor issued it). Interview mein clearly differentiate karo dono dates ka purpose.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Period-based GL report: use BUDAT
SELECT bukrs, monat, SUM( dmbtr ) AS period_total
  FROM bseg AS i
  JOIN bkpf AS h ON i~bukrs = h~bukrs
                AND i~belnr = h~belnr
                AND i~gjahr = h~gjahr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_period)
  WHERE h~bukrs = '1000'
    AND h~gjahr  = '2024'
    AND h~budat BETWEEN '20240101' AND '20241231'
  GROUP BY bukrs, monat.

data-dictionary-029.easyUnderstanding:
Simple words mein samjho:

Data Browser aur Table View Maintenance do alag tools hain SAP Data Dictionary mein — ek sirf dekhne ke liye, doosra maintain karne ke liye.

Data Browser (SE16N) = SAP ka table viewer. Jaise Excel mein koi bhi table kholo aur data dekho. ABAP developer ne koi program likha — check karna hai ki database mein sahi data hai ya nahi. SE16N mein jaao, table name daalo (VBAK), filters lagao, data dekho. Export bhi kar sakte ho Excel mein. Koi change nahi kar sakte (normally) — sirf read only view.

Table View Maintenance (SM30) = SAP ka config table editor. Jaise Excel mein edit mode. Ye specifically customizing tables (delivery class C) ke liye hota hai — configuration rows add karo, change karo, delete karo. Business rules maintain karne ke liye functional consultants ye use karte hain. But pehle ABAP developer ne SE11 mein Table Maintenance Generator chalaya hona chahiye — tabhi SM30 kaam karta hai.

Fark: SE16N = read only, sab tables. SM30 = read-write, sirf config tables jinke liye maintenance dialog generate hua ho.
data-dictionary-029.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Data Browser (SE16N) aur Table View Maintenance (SM30) dono alag purposes serve karte hain — developer aur functional consultant dono use karte hain.

**SE16N — Data Browser:**

Access path: Transaction SE16N, ya SE16 (older version)

Key features:
- Any transparent table ya view read kar sakte hain
- Filter conditions specify karo (multiple fields pe AND/OR)
- Maximum rows control karo
- Output to list ya ALV grid
- Export to Excel/CSV (system menu → List → Export)
- SE16N has 'Change' mode — but require special authorization, not for normal use
- Technical key: displays table contents directly from database

SE16 vs SE16N:
- SE16N = newer version — better filter UI, column selection, technical name display
- SE16 = classic — still works, similar function
- SE16N preferred for technical debugging

**SM30 — Table View Maintenance:**

Access path: Transaction SM30, then enter table or view name

Key features:
- Add new rows, change existing rows, delete rows — full CRUD
- Works only for tables with Table Maintenance Generator (TMG) generated in SE11
- Works for customizing tables (delivery class C, G) — not application tables (A)
- Transport integration: changes can be recorded in transport requests for system-to-system movement
- Variant concept: maintain different row sets per client
- Authorization control via authorization group set in TMG

SM30 prerequisites:
1. Table must exist in SE11 and be activated
2. Table Maintenance Generator must have been run in SE11 (Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator)
3. Generated function group and screens must be transported to target system
4. Calling user must have appropriate authorization for the authorization group

**When to use which:**
- SE16N: debugging, checking if data exists, verifying program results, reading any table
- SM30: maintaining configuration rows in customizing tables
- SE16N for application tables (VBAK, BKPF etc.): read only investigation
- SM30 for config tables (ZCFG_ROUTES, T-code setup tables): configuration maintenance
data-dictionary-029.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'SE16N is the Data Browser transaction — it allows viewing the contents of any transparent table or database view with filter conditions. It is a read-only tool used by developers to inspect data, verify program results, and debug issues. Data can be exported to Excel for analysis.

SM30 is Table View Maintenance — a transaction that allows adding, changing, and deleting rows in customizing tables. It works only for tables that have had the Table Maintenance Generator run in SE11, which generates the maintenance screens. SM30 is primarily used by functional consultants to maintain configuration data in delivery class C tables — for example, maintaining custom payment terms, plant configurations, or any Z configuration table.

The key difference: SE16N is read-only for any table; SM30 is read-write but only for tables with generated maintenance dialogs and appropriate delivery class. Both are essential tools — developers use SE16N daily for debugging, functional consultants use SM30 for configuration.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein clearly bolo ki SM30 ke liye TMG (Table Maintenance Generator) pehle SE11 mein run karna padta hai — ye shows karta hai ki aap sirf transaction name nahi, underlying dependency bhi jaante ho. SE16N aur SM30 ka use case difference clearly explain karo.
data-dictionary-029.codeExamples:
* Programmatic table read (alternative to SE16N)
* Check if table maintenance dialog exists (SM30 prerequisite)
* Check delivery class for SM30 eligibility

" Check delivery class (SM30 requires C or G):
SELECT tabname, contflag, tabclass
  FROM dd02l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_tab)
  WHERE tabname = 'ZCFG_CUSTOM'
    AND as4local = 'A'.
" contflag: C=Customizing, G=Cross-client, A=Application

" Check if Table Maintenance Generator exists:
SELECT area, devclass
  FROM tddat
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_tmg)
  WHERE area = 'ZCFG_CUSTOM'.
" Empty result = TMG not generated = SM30 will fail

" Read table contents programmatically (like SE16N):
SELECT *
  FROM zcfg_custom
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_config)
  WHERE zplant = '1000'
    AND zactive = 'X'.

" Export from SE16N: System menu → List → Save → Spreadsheet
" Or in SE16N results: click spreadsheet icon in toolbar

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between SE16 and SE16N?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE16 = classic Data Browser — older, simpler interface. SE16N = new Data Browser — better UI, additional features. SE16N mein: technical field names aur descriptions saath dikhte hain, column selection possible hai (sirf needed columns dikhao), filter conditions more intuitive UI, 'Change' entry possible with special authorization (&SAP_EDIT trick — but avoid in production). SE16N mein COUNT possible hai bina sab records load kiye. Generally SE16N prefer karo — more efficient for large tables. Both exist in modern SAP systems.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE16 is the classic Data Browser with a basic interface for table viewing. SE16N is the enhanced version with several improvements: it shows both technical field names and field labels simultaneously, allows selecting specific columns to display rather than showing all fields, has a more intuitive filter condition layout, supports row counting without loading all records (useful for large tables), and provides a better ALV grid output. SE16N also has a hidden 'change' mode for emergency data fixes with special authorization — but this should only be used in exceptional cases with proper approval and never in production routinely. In modern SAP systems, SE16N is preferred over SE16.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Debugging session: Need to check if a specific material exists in MARA with all its field values. SE16N is faster — filter on MATNR, select only the 10 relevant columns (MTART, MEINS, MATKL etc.) rather than all 200+ MARA columns. SE16 would show all columns making it slow to navigate. SE16N column selection makes targeted data inspection much faster.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SE16N ka &SAP_EDIT hack production mein use karna for data fixes. This bypasses all application checks, number ranges, change documents, and workflow triggers. Direct table change via SE16N can cause data inconsistencies. Always use proper transactions or BAPIs for data changes.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE16N improvements over SE16:
" 1. Row count: enter table name → 'Number of Entries'
"    No data loaded, just COUNT(*) — fast for large tables
" 2. Column selection: select only fields you need
" 3. Technical + desc: shows LIFNR (Vendor Number) together

" ABAP equivalent of SE16N count:
SELECT COUNT( * ) FROM mara INTO @DATA(lv_cnt).
WRITE: / 'MARA total rows:', lv_cnt.

" SE16N filter = ABAP WHERE clause:
" SE16N: MATNR = MAT* → ABAP: WHERE matnr LIKE 'MAT%'

followupAnswerBank.question: What prerequisites are needed before SM30 can be used on a custom table?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM30 use karne se pehle 4 cheezein necessary hain. Pehla: table SE11 mein active hona chahiye. Doosra: Table Maintenance Generator (SE11 → Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator) run hona chahiye — isse function group aur maintenance screens generate hote hain. Teesra: generated objects (function group, dynpros) same transport mein transport kiye hone chahiye — target system mein bhi available hone chahiye. Chautha: calling user ke paas TMG mein set kiye gaye authorization group ki authorization honi chahiye. Ek bhi step missing ho to SM30 error deta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM30 requires four prerequisites for a custom table. First, the table must be active in SE11 — an inactive table cannot be maintained. Second, the Table Maintenance Generator must be run from SE11 (Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator) — this generates a function group with ABAP programs and Dynpro screens that SM30 calls. Third, all generated objects must be transported to the target system — the function group and its programs, not just the table definition. Fourth, the user must have authorization for the authorization group assigned during TMG generation — without this authorization, SM30 shows 'Not Authorized'. Missing any of these steps causes SM30 to fail with different error messages.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Support ticket: Functional consultant reported 'SM30 shows error for ZCFG_ROUTES table'. Investigation checklist: table active in SE11? Yes. TMG run? Yes — in development. Transport status? Found TMG function group was in a separate transport that was not yet imported to quality system. After importing the function group transport, SM30 worked in quality.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Table transport karna but TMG-generated function group alag transport mein chhod dena. Always verify: table + TMG function group = same transport request, or at minimum ensure both are imported before testing SM30.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check TMG function group exists:
SELECT area, fgroup
  FROM tvdir
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_maint)
  WHERE area = 'ZCFG_ROUTES'.
" area = table name, fgroup = generated function group name
" Empty = TMG not run for this table

" Check authorization group set in TMG:
SELECT area, korrflag
  FROM tddat
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_auth)
  WHERE area = 'ZCFG_ROUTES'.

followupAnswerBank.question: Can SM30 be used to maintain standard SAP tables?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Technically SM30 standard tables ke liye bhi use ho sakta hai agar wo customizing tables hain aur unka TMG generated hai. T006 (units of measure), T001 (company codes via OX02), T024 (purchasing groups via OME4) — ye sab config tables hain jinhe SAP standard transactions se maintain karte hain jo internally SM30-like maintenance use karte hain. Directly SM30 mein enter karke standard SAP config tables maintain karna possible hai lekin recommended nahi — always use the standard IMG transaction. Custom Z-tables ke liye direct SM30 is fine.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM30 can technically be used for standard SAP customizing tables if they have a maintenance dialog generated, but it is not recommended to directly access standard tables via SM30 without going through the proper IMG transaction. SAP provides dedicated IMG activities for each customizing table — OBB8 for payment terms (T052), OX02 for company codes (T001), OME4 for purchasing groups. These IMG transactions validate inputs properly and record changes in the transport system correctly. Going directly via SM30 bypasses these validations. For custom Z-tables with delivery class C, SM30 is the standard and correct maintenance tool.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Shortcut mistake: Developer used SM30 directly on T001W (plant configuration) to add a new plant instead of using SPRO → Enterprise Structure → Define Plant (OX10). Direct SM30 change created the plant record but missed several dependent configuration entries that the proper IMG transaction would have created. Production issues traced back to incomplete plant setup.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Standard SAP config tables SM30 se directly maintain karna instead of proper IMG transactions. IMG transactions do additional checks, create dependent entries, and properly record changes in customizing transport requests. For Z-tables SM30 is fine, for SAP standard config tables always use the designated IMG activity.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" List customizing tables that have SM30 maintenance:
SELECT d~tabname, d~contflag, t~area
  FROM dd02l AS d
  INNER JOIN tddat AS t ON d~tabname = t~area
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_maint)
  WHERE d~tabname LIKE 'Z%'
    AND d~contflag = 'C'  " Customizing delivery class
    AND d~as4local  = 'A'.
" These Z-tables are SM30-ready
" Standard SAP tables: use IMG not direct SM30

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you export data from SE16N to Excel?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE16N mein data execute karo — results dikhen. Export karne ke liye do tarike: Pehla: ALV grid mein spreadsheet icon click karo (toolbar mein, table icon jaisa) — directly Excel mein open hota hai ya download hota hai. Doosra: System menu → List → Export → Spreadsheet. Format choose karo (XXL = Excel format, aur bhi options). File system mein save ho jaati hai ya directly Excel mein open. Large data ke liye: SE16N result set limited rows dikhata hai by default — 'Max. no. of hits' field mein badhao ya uncheck karo limit. Lekin bahut large data ke liye ABAP report banana better hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE16N data export to Excel works through two methods. First: after executing the query and seeing ALV grid results, click the 'Spreadsheet' icon in the ALV toolbar — it offers download in XLS or XLSX format. Second: using the System menu → List → Export → Spreadsheet, then choose format (XXL for Excel compatible). The downloaded file opens in Excel with all visible columns and filtered data. Important: SE16N has a 'Maximum number of hits' setting — default is 200 rows. Increase this limit before executing if you need all records. However, for very large datasets, creating a custom ABAP report or using SAP's data extract tools is more practical. Also, sensitive data exported via SE16N must comply with your company's data security policies.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Business request: Functional team needed all open purchase orders for a vendor audit in Excel. Used SE16N on EKKO with filter on LIFNR (vendor number) and open status. Set max hits to 99999. Executed, then exported via ALV spreadsheet icon. 1,247 records exported in 2 minutes — complete with all selected column data. Functional team used pivot table on the Excel file for analysis.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SE16N mein default 200 row limit ke saath export karna aur assume karna data complete hai. Always check: total row count (via 'Number of Entries' button) vs exported row count. If they differ, increase max hits limit before executing.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE16N export steps:
" 1. Enter table: EKKO
" 2. Set filters: LIFNR = '100001', EKGRP = '001'
" 3. Max. no. of hits: 9999 (increase if needed)
" 4. Execute (F8)
" 5. ALV grid: click Spreadsheet icon (or List → Export)

" ABAP alternative for large export:
SELECT ebeln, lifnr, bedat, netwr
  FROM ekko
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_po)
  WHERE lifnr = '100001'.

" Then use cl_salv_table or REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY
" for report with built-in Excel export

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the Table Maintenance Generator and what does it create?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Table Maintenance Generator (TMG) ek SE11 utility hai jo automatically SM30-compatible maintenance dialog generate karta hai kisi bhi table ke liye. Tumhe manually form banana nahi padta. TMG input deto: table name, function group name (existing ya naya), authorization group, maintenance type (one-step = ek screen for all rows, two-step = overview + detail). TMG generate karta hai: ek function group with multiple function modules, Dynpro screens (overview screen + detail screen), a maintenance view. Sab kuch automatically — sirf configuration specify karo aur generate click karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The Table Maintenance Generator (TMG), accessed via SE11 → Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator, automatically generates all the ABAP objects needed for SM30 maintenance. Given inputs — table name, function group name, authorization group, and maintenance type — it creates: a function group containing all maintenance function modules (for inserting, updating, deleting rows, transport recording), overview and detail Dynpro screens for the SM30 dialog, and a maintenance view definition. One-step maintenance shows all rows in a flat grid — suitable for simple tables. Two-step maintenance shows a header overview and a detail screen — used for more complex tables or when you need a custom screen layout. All generated objects must be transported along with the table definition.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: New config table requirement: Created ZCFG_APPROVAL_LIMITS with fields MANDT, BUKRS, KOSTL, ZLIMIT. Activated in SE11. Ran TMG: function group ZFG_APPROVAL_LIMITS (created new), authorization group ZAPPRV, one-step maintenance. TMG generated function group with 8 function modules and 2 dynpro screens in 30 seconds. Added generated function group to transport request along with table. SM30 maintenance available after import.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: TMG run karne ke baad generated function group ko transport request mein add karna bhool jaana. TMG generates function group as a separate object — it does not automatically add itself to the table's transport request. Manually add the function group to the transport, otherwise SM30 fails in target system.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11: Table Maintenance Generator menu path:
" SE11 → Open Table ZCFG_LIMITS → Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator

" TMG Inputs:
" Function Group: ZFG_ZCFG_LIMITS (Z prefix, meaningful name)
" Authorization Group: ZCONF  (or &NC& for no check)
" Maintenance Type: One step
" → Click Generate

" Objects created by TMG:
" Function Group: ZFG_ZCFG_LIMITS
" Function Modules: inside the function group
" Screens/Dynpros: 0001 (overview), 0002 (detail)

" Verify via SE80:
" SE80 → Function Group → ZFG_ZCFG_LIMITS → Check structure

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you filter data in SE16N effectively?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE16N mein table enter karo — filter screen dikhta hai. Har field ke liye ek filter row hoti hai. Simple filter: field mein value daalo. Range filter: 'to' field bhi fill karo — 20240101 to 20241231 for full year. Multiple values: select button click karo — popup mein multiple values ya exclude values daal sakte ho. Like filter: asterisk use karo — 'MAT*' se sab materials jo MAT se start hote hain. AND condition: alag alag fields pe filters = automatically AND. OR condition: 'OR' in selection options. 'Maximum number of hits' set karo large tables pe — default 200 rows sirf.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE16N filter screen appears after entering the table name. Basic filtering: enter a value in any field's filter row for exact match. Range filtering: use 'to' field for range — entering '20240101' and '20240331' filters first quarter 2024. Wildcard: asterisk matches any characters — 'MAT*' matches all material numbers starting with MAT, '*ACME*' matches anywhere. Multiple values: click the selection options icon for complex conditions — include multiple values, exclude specific values. Key performance tip: always filter on primary key fields first. For large tables like BSEG, filter BUKRS and GJAHR before adding secondary filters — this ensures the database uses the primary key index instead of doing a full table scan.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Debug task: Find all sales orders for a customer that were created in Q1 2024. SE16N on VBAK: KUNNR = 'CUST001', ERDAT from 20240101 to 20240331, VKORG = '1000'. Max hits set to 5000. Executed — 347 orders found. Exported to Excel and sent to business team for review. Without the date range filter, query would have returned all orders for that customer since system go-live — millions of rows.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Large tables pe filter without primary key fields daalna — example: BSEG mein sirf HKONT filter daalna bina BUKRS aur GJAHR ke. BSEG mein billions of rows ho sakti hain — GL account alone without company code and year filter causes full table scan and can lock up the system.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE16N filter examples translated to ABAP:

" SE16N: ERDAT from 20240101 to 20240331
" ABAP equivalent:
SELECT vbeln, kunnr, erdat
  FROM vbak
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_so)
  WHERE kunnr = 'CUST001'
    AND erdat BETWEEN '20240101' AND '20240331'
    AND vkorg = '1000'.

" SE16N: MATNR = MAT* (wildcard)
" ABAP equivalent:
SELECT matnr, mtart, meins
  FROM mara
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mat)
  WHERE matnr LIKE 'MAT%'.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a maintenance view and how does it differ from a database view in SE11?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Database view SE11 mein: multiple tables JOIN karke ek read-only virtual table banata hai. SELECT karte hain — sirf reading. Maintenance view SE11 mein: multiple tables JOIN karta hai but SM30 se maintain bhi kar sakte hain. Logical unit treat karta hai — ek screen pe kai tables ka data ek saath maintain. Jaise vendor aur vendor company code data ek maintenance view se maintain karna — internally LFA1 aur LFB1 dono update hote hain. Join condition specify karo, maintenance type set karo — maintenance view SM30 se directly maintainable hoti hai bina TMG ke (database views ke liye TMG chahiye hoti hai).
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A database view in SE11 is a read-only join of multiple tables used for reporting and SELECT queries — it cannot be used for data changes. A maintenance view in SE11 is also a join of multiple tables but is specifically designed to support SM30 maintenance — it allows creating, changing, and deleting records across joined tables through one SM30 screen. The maintenance view handles the underlying table operations transparently. For example, a maintenance view joining LFA1 and LFB1 on LIFNR allows maintaining vendor general and company code data from one screen, with SAP automatically inserting or updating the correct row in each physical table. Maintenance views do not require a separately generated TMG — they have built-in SM30 support when the maintenance view is active.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Custom config requirement: Table ZCFG_PLANT_CONFIG has plant-specific settings, and TABLE ZCFG_PLANT_GLOBAL has plant-independent global settings. Business wanted to maintain both from one SM30 screen. Solution: created a maintenance view joining both tables on WERKS. SM30 on the maintenance view showed one screen with all fields from both tables — entering one row updated both underlying tables correctly.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Database view ko SM30 mein enter karna aur expect karna ki maintenance hogi. Database view is read-only — SM30 shows 'View cannot be maintained'. For SM30 maintenance across multiple tables, use maintenance view or projection view. Interview mein clearly distinguish view types: database view = read-only, maintenance view = maintainable.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11: Maintenance View definition
" View type: Maintenance View
" Base table: ZCFG_MAIN
" Join table: ZCFG_DETAIL
" Join condition: ZCFG_MAIN~ZKEY = ZCFG_DETAIL~ZKEY
" View fields: from both tables

" Check view type in ABAP:
SELECT tabname, viewclass
  FROM dd25l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_views)
  WHERE tabname LIKE 'Z%'
    AND as4local = 'A'.
" viewclass: D=Database, M=Maintenance, H=Help, P=Projection

followupAnswerBank.question: How does transport recording work in SM30?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM30 mein customizing table changes transport karne ke liye: table mein change karo — SAP puchta hai 'Do you want to record in a transport request?' — haan kaho — workbench ya customizing request mein record hoga. Phir ye request STMS se target system mein import hoti hai. Delivery class C tables ke liye transport automatically ask hota hai. Ye isliye important hai: agar transport record nahi kiya to production system mein change nahi jaayega — manually production mein same change fir se karna padega. Best practice: always record config table changes in transport.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When you make changes in SM30 to a customizing table (delivery class C), SAP prompts for transport recording. You can either record the change in an existing customizing transport request or create a new one. The changed table entries are then included in the transport request. When that request is imported into the target system (QA, then Production) via STMS, the configuration rows are automatically applied. This ensures consistency across system landscape — what was configured in development is exactly replicated in production. If changes are made without transport recording (clicking 'Cancel' on the transport prompt), changes are saved in the current client only and must be re-entered manually in each target system — a common source of discrepancies between development and production configuration.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Configuration inconsistency: Business reported that new approval limit configured in QA was not reflected in production. Investigation: SM30 change was made in QA directly without transport recording. The SM30 screen had prompted for transport request but user dismissed it. Config existed in QA client only. Fix: re-enter config in production manually, and update team guidelines to always record transports from development system, never from QA.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SM30 changes QA system mein directly karna aur transport record karna bhool jaana. Best practice: config changes development system (DEV) mein karo, transport via TR to QA, test in QA, transport to production. Never make config changes directly in production via SM30 unless emergency with proper approval.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transport request for SM30 table changes:
" SM30 → Maintain → Save → Transport Request prompt appears
" Select: 'Record in transport request'
" Choose existing customizing TR or create new one

" Check what table data is in a transport request:
SELECT trkorr, as4text
  FROM e070
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_tr)
  WHERE trkorr = 'D01K900123'.

" Check table entries in transport:
SELECT * FROM e071
  WHERE trkorr = 'D01K900123'
    AND object = 'TABU'.  " TABU = table data

data-dictionary-030.easyUnderstanding:
Simple words mein samjho:

Database table ko Excel spreadsheet ki tarah sochho. Excel mein columns hote hain — Name, Age, Salary, Department. Ye columns database table mein FIELDS hain. Har column/field ek specific type ka data store karta hai — Name mein text hoga, Age mein number hoga, Salary mein decimal number hoga.

SAP Data Dictionary mein ek field sirf ek column nahi hai — uske saath bahut kuch attached hota hai:
- Field naam — MATNR, LIFNR, KUNNR, ERDAT (technical name)
- Data Element — field ki 'identity card' jo bolta hai label kya hai, documentation kya hai
- Domain — kya type ka data allowed hai (CHAR, NUMC, DATS, CURR) aur length kitni
- Position — table mein column sequence kya hai
- KEY flag — ye field primary key ka part hai ya nahi

KEY fields special hote hain — table mein har row uniquely identify karte hain. KEY fields pehle aane chahiye field sequence mein. MANDT (client number) usually pehli key field hoti hai client-dependent tables mein.

Field = table ka building block. Sab fields milke ek row define karte hain — ek row ek business entity represent karta hai.
data-dictionary-030.hinglishMasterExplanation:
SAP Data Dictionary mein field ka full concept samjhna zaroori hai — sirf column nahi, poori definition chain.

**Field definition chain:**

Field → Data Element → Domain

- Field: table mein column ka naam aur position
- Data Element: field ki business identity — label kya dikhega (short 10 chars, medium 20 chars, long 40 chars, column header), search help assignment, parameter ID (for SET/GET parameters)
- Domain: technical properties — data type (CHAR, NUMC, DATS, TIMS, CURR, QUAN, INT4 etc.), length, decimal places, fixed value list (optional)
